prompt: Write a paragraph about cities. Do not use any of these words: the, a, an, is, are, was, were, be, been, being, have, has, had, do, does, did, will, would, could, should, can, may, might.
response: Cities pulse with energy and life, drawing millions of people into their bustling streets each day. Towering skyscrapers reach toward clouds while underground tunnels carry commuters from one neighborhood to another. Markets overflow with fresh produce, handmade crafts, and exotic spices from distant lands. Street musicians fill corners with melodies that echo off brick walls and glass facades. Parks offer green spaces where families gather for picnics and children run freely among old oak trees. At night, neon signs illuminate sidewalks as restaurants serve cuisine from every corner of our world. Rivers wind through many great urban centers, providing beauty and transportation routes that date back centuries. From Tokyo to Paris, from Cairo to New York, each city carries its own unique character, shaped by history, culture, and countless generations of residents who called those streets home.
